report_name: UK Announces Major Cyber Law Overhaul, Including Reforms to Computer Misuse Act

identities.name: UK Government
identities.description: Announced a legislative agenda to reform its digital and cyber regulatory framework, including a new Cyber Security and Resilience Bill and reforms to the 1990 Computer Misuse Act (CMA).

identities.name: European Union
identities.description: Originator of the Network and Information Security (NIS 2) directive, which the new UK Cyber Security and Resilience Bill is expected to align with.

identities.name: Critical Infrastructure Providers
identities.description: A class of organizations that will face new, more stringent security obligations under the proposed UK Cyber Security and Resilience Bill.

identities.name: IT Service Providers
identities.description: Managed Service Providers (MSPs) and other critical IT suppliers that will be brought under the scope of new UK national cybersecurity regulations.

identities.name: Data Center Operators
identities.description: A class of organizations that will be placed under new security obligations by the Cyber Security and Resilience Bill due to their foundational role in the digital economy.

identities.name: UK Cybersecurity Industry
identities.description: Cybersecurity professionals, researchers, and companies in the UK who will be impacted by the Computer Misuse Act (CMA) reforms, which aim to provide a statutory defense for legitimate security activities.
